title: QUIC RFC 9001 Stateless Reset Disposition Matrix Architecture
purpose: Define the design boundary for the helper-owned stateless-reset formatting-failure branch after AEAD-limit terminal discard, keeping emission suppression exact when formatting inputs are malformed.
risks: If helper-format failures are not kept separate from emitted outcomes, callers can overclaim response success.
If host shells reconstruct their own formatting rules, the matrix can drift away from the shared helper.
If the retained-route floor and the formatting-failure branch are merged, later trace slices can lose their boundary.
design_summary: The runtime keeps stateless-reset formatting in a shared helper and treats invalid formatting inputs as a non-emitted branch. The endpoint helper remains the single place that formats a datagram, and the endpoint-host and listener-host paths continue to delegate through that helper rather than inventing their own formatting logic. This architecture keeps the retained-route floor from ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0004 intact while making the formatting-failure branch explicit and suppressive instead of success-biased.
alternatives_considered: Allow each host shell to format its own stateless reset datagram. Rejected because it would create divergent matrix behavior and duplicate validation.
Fold malformed-format handling into the retained-route floor without a separate slice. Rejected because the failure branch is a distinct helper outcome and should remain traceable on its own.
Treat any helper failure as an emitted reset. Rejected because it would fabricate responses from invalid inputs.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Invalid token length must suppress emission.
Undersized datagram length or destination space must suppress emission.
An empty version-profile snapshot must suppress emission.
Only a fully formatted datagram may be reported as emitted.
The retained-route floor from ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0004 remains a subset of this matrix.
data_and_state_considerations: The formatting branch uses only the helper inputs, not a broader route lookup. Token bytes, destination length, and retained version-profile snapshot are the relevant state inputs; if any of them are malformed, the helper returns a non-emitted result and the caller drops the response path. The endpoint and host shells should not cache a parallel formatting outcome.
open_questions: If a future ambiguity-specific route model appears, it should split into its own gap instead of broadening this helper failure slice.
